title: Parenteral Anticoagulants
body_html:
Heparin therapy is monitored using the activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT).

Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) is the development of a low platelet count following initiation of heparin therapy. It is caused by the formation of abnormal antibodies that activate platelets.

Pharmacology
Cardiovascular pharmacology
Heparin therapy is monitored using the activated partial thromboplastin time (aPTT).

Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) is the development of a low platelet count following initiation of heparin therapy. It is caused by the formation of abnormal antibodies that activate platelets.
